name: The 1950s
description: Rock 'n' roll, Cold War culture, and the dawn of suburbia

questions.q: Elvis Presley is famously known by which royal nickname?

source.notes: Often simply 'The King' or 'The King of Rock and Roll'.

questions.q: Which band had a 1954 mega-hit with 'Rock Around the Clock'?

source.notes: Often cited as the song that launched mainstream rock 'n' roll.

questions.q: Which flamboyant 1950s rock 'n' roller was born Richard Penniman?

source.notes: Hits included 'Tutti Frutti', 'Long Tall Sally', and 'Good Golly, Miss Molly'.

questions.q: Bing Crosby scored a perennial hit with which seasonal song?

source.notes: First released 1942 but continued to top charts through the 50s; all-time best-selling single.

questions.q: In what year did Queen Elizabeth II ascend to the British throne?

source.notes: Succeeded her father George VI on February 6, 1952; coronation in June 1953.

questions.q: Which former WWII general served as U.S. President for most of the 1950s?

source.notes: President 1953–1961; Republican.

questions.q: In what year did Buddy Holly die in a plane crash later dubbed 'The Day the Music Died'?

source.notes: February 3, 1959 near Clear Lake, Iowa; also killed Ritchie Valens and J.P. Richardson (The Big Bopper).

questions.q: In what year did the first annual Grammy Awards take place?

source.notes: First held May 4, 1959, honoring achievements of 1958.

questions.q: Which instrument did New Orleans rock 'n' roll legend Fats Domino famously play?

source.notes: Hits include 'Blueberry Hill' and 'Ain't That a Shame'.

questions.q: Who originally wrote and recorded 'Blue Suede Shoes' in 1955, before Elvis covered it?

source.notes: Perkins's original hit the charts in January 1956; Elvis covered it later that year.

questions.q: What was the name of the satellite launched by the Soviet Union in October 1957 — the first artificial Earth satellite?

source.notes: Launched October 4, 1957; triggered the 'Space Race'.

questions.q: What were the first names of the sibling duo the Everly Brothers?

source.notes: Hits included 'Wake Up Little Susie' and 'All I Have to Do Is Dream'.

questions.q: Which Chuck Berry song, released in 1956, urged a classical composer to 'roll over'?

source.notes: Later covered famously by The Beatles on 'With the Beatles' (1963).

questions.q: What was the title of Little Richard's breakthrough 1955 hit single?

source.notes: Released November 1955; famous for the intro 'A-wop-bop-a-loo-bop-a-lop-bam-boom!'

questions.q: Which Texas-born singer was nicknamed 'The Big O'?

source.notes: Known for his operatic voice and signature dark sunglasses.

questions.q: Which wild 1950s piano-playing rock 'n' roller was nicknamed 'The Killer'?

source.notes: Known for 'Great Balls of Fire' and 'Whole Lotta Shakin' Goin' On'.

questions.q: The original Disneyland theme park opened in which U.S. state in 1955?

source.notes: Opened July 17, 1955, in Anaheim, California. Walt Disney World (Florida) opened 1971.

questions.q: Which 17-year-old Mexican-American rock 'n' roller had his first hit with 'Donna' before dying in the 1959 plane crash with Buddy Holly?

source.notes: Born Ricardo Valenzuela; 'La Bamba' was the B-side of 'Donna'.

questions.q: Who had a 1950 US No. 1 hit with 'Mona Lisa'?

source.notes: Composed by Jay Livingston and Ray Evans for the 1950 film 'Captain Carey, U.S.A.'

questions.q: Ray Charles was born under which real surname, which he later dropped to avoid confusion with a boxer?

source.notes: Born Ray Charles Robinson; dropped his surname to avoid confusion with boxer Sugar Ray Robinson.

questions.q: The armistice ending active combat in the Korean War was signed in which year?

source.notes: Signed at Panmunjom on July 27, 1953 — no formal peace treaty was ever concluded.

questions.q: 'Splish Splash' was the first million-selling record for which singer, who later had a hit with 'Mack the Knife'?

source.notes: Released 1958; Darin co-wrote the song. Died in 1973 at 37.
